item.title: Blue Owl, HPS Join Private Credit Funds Stung by February Losses
item.description: Private credit funds already under strain from heavy redemptions have a new pain point: losses in February are shaping up as the worst in more than three years.

item.title: Quantum Firm Xanadu Jumps After Rollercoaster Debut Trading
item.description: Canadian startup Xanadu Quantum Technologies Inc. rallied after a rocky start in public markets, buffeted by souring sentiment toward equities.

item.title: PE-Backed Convenience Store Operator Yesway Files for US IPO
item.description: Yesway Inc., a convenience store and gas station operator focused on rural communities in the Midwest and the Southwest, filed for an initial public offering.

item.title: State Street’s Tiny Fee Cut Is Paying Off Big Time
item.description: How a small fee cut on SPYM attracted billions.

item.title: Ceconomy Says Austria May Block JD.com’s Purchase Offer
item.description: Austrian authorities have so far declined to approve Chinese e-commerce firm JD.com Inc’s offer to acquire Germany’s Ceconomy AG, throwing the broader €2.2 billion ($2.5 billion) deal for Europe’s largest consumer electronics into doubt.

item.title: Citadel’s Barratt Departs After Losses on Spirit, Other Trades
item.description: The giant multistrategy hedge fund Citadel parted ways with credit portfolio manager Zachariah Barratt after a series of losses, including a soured bet on Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc., according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Markets Are Underpricing Energy Shock Risks, Warns Barclays President
item.description: Barclays Bank PLC’s President, Stephen Dainton, says investors in US markets may be underestimating the risks of high energy prices and rising interest rates.

item.title: Telecom Italia Prepares to Scrap Inwit Renewal at Sunday Meeting
item.description: Telecom Italia SpA is preparing to scrap the renewal of a mobile-phone tower agreement with Inwit SpA that would have extended the contract through 2038, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Moon-Bound Astronauts Arrive in Florida Ahead of NASA Mission
item.description: The four astronauts of NASA’s historic Artemis II mission have arrived in Florida, days ahead of their planned launch that will send them around the moon and deeper into space than any humans have gone before.

item.title: Fliers Book $34,000 Private Jets to Escape TSA Hell at Airports
item.description: As TSA chaos spreads through US airports, some travelers are shelling out thousands of dollars to skip the lines altogether.

item.title: Newsom Bars Officials From Insider Trading on Prediction Markets
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom is barring high-ranking state officials from using inside information to wager in prediction markets.

item.title: Brooklyn Preschool Rocked by Ex-Director’s Alleged Embezzlement
item.description: The KinderHaus preschool sits tucked away in a quiet corner of Brooklyn’s southern Park Slope neighborhood. Some parents have called it one of the best kept secrets in the borough, allowing them to immerse their children in German from an early age. Cost? Nearly $50,000 a year.

item.title: Kalshi Approved for Margin Trading as It Lures Wall Street Pros
item.description: Kalshi Inc. has secured a license allowing it to offer margin trading to users, a feature that would make the prediction market platform more appealing to sophisticated institutional investors.

item.title: France Unveils Limited Aid to Offset Iran War Impact on Economy
item.description: France’s government said it would provide a €70 million ($80.6 million) of aid to a limited number of sectors over a short period as it seeks to cushion the economy from the impact of the Iran war without straining public finances.

item.title: Oracle’s Credit Risk Measure Nears Record High on AI-Debt Fears
item.description: A gauge of Oracle Corp.’s credit risk is nearing an all-time closing high on Friday, the latest sign that investors are growing increasingly wary about the technology giant’s heavy debt load amid rising oil prices and falling stock prices.

item.title: Merz Signals Germany to Take Stake in Franco-German Tank Maker
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz hinted that the German government would purchase a stake in Franco-German tank manufacturer KNDS NV as part of an effort to secure influence in the defense venture.

item.title: Russia Set to Ban Gasoline Exports From April 1 as Prices Jump
item.description: Russia plans to ban gasoline exports for the nation’s producers from April 1 to meet domestic demand as global fuel prices surge amid the war in Iran.

item.title: Goldman Traders Warn Against Shorting Stocks Amid ‘Upside’ Risk
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s trading desk is warning investors not to turn bearish on US stocks, saying current positioning leaves the market vulnerable to a short squeeze if geopolitical tensions ease.

item.title: Total Inks Deal to Use EDF’s Nuclear Power for French Refineries
item.description: Oil major TotalEnergies SE, which is diversifying into renewable and gas-fired electricity output, struck a 12-year deal to buy Electricite de France SA’s nuclear power to feed its French refineries and chemical sites.

item.title: The Potential for Real Misery
item.description: The stagflation of the 1970s wasn’t just an oil shock.

item.title: European Commission’s Data Stolen in Hack on AWS Account
item.description: The European Commission was hit by a cyberattack that may have resulted in the theft of internal data, months after another incident potentially exposed some staff details.

item.title: EPA Finalizes Higher Biofuels Quota to Bolster Domestic Demand
item.description: The Trump administration unveiled long-awaited biofuels blending standards, giving a stronger mandate than a plan proposed last year in a further show of support for American farmers and biofuels producers.

item.title: It Sucks to Work in the Video-Game Industry Right Now
item.description: Even developers who successfully release big hits, like Fortnite and Battlefield, are losing their jobs

item.title: ECB Shouldn’t Rush Iran Response or Overreact, Schnabel Says
item.description: The European Central Bank shouldn’t rush its response to the Iran war and must be careful not to “overreact,” Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel said, while urging officials to remain agile and vigilant.

item.title: Paraguay’s Economy Grew 6.6% in 2025, Fastest Pace in 12 Years
item.description: The land-locked South American country posted its highest growth in more than a decade last year, with the economy expanding 6.6%.

item.title: First Brands to Sell 12 Brands Including Autolite for $25 Million
item.description: Bankrupt auto-parts maker First Brands Group agreed to sell some of its most-recognizable brands for $25 million after losing rescue funding and major customers.

item.title: Brightline Florida Said to Work With Adviser on Troubled Railroad’s Debt Talks
item.description: Brightline Trains Florida LLC, the struggling private rail line connecting Orlando to Miami, is working with Perella Weinberg Partners on a potential debt restructuring and equity raise, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: India Acts to Curb Forex Speculation as Rupee Plumbs New Lows
item.description: India’s central bank stepped up its defense of the battered rupee, rolling out a measure to curb speculation after it slid to a new low amid concerns of a widening trade gap driven by the US-Iran war.

item.title: Surging Oil, Gold Prices Lift Toronto Stocks as S&P 500 Drops
item.description: Canadian stocks rose Friday on higher gold and oil prices, defying the swoon across major US and European indexes as traders price in the potential for a longer Iran war.

item.title: Chile Inflation Expectations Spike to Highest Since March 2023
item.description: Chile’s one-year breakevens — a measure of inflation expectations — jumped to the highest since March 2023 on Friday after the government loosened a fuel stabilization mechanism, triggering a jump in gasoline prices.
